rows.definition: Adjudicating is the act of evaluating a proposed tool call (or a buffered turn of them) against the loaded policy and refusal rungs to produce an admit/refuse verdict, exercised on the gateway's served fast path (adjudicateProposedServed), its full-turn gate (adjudicateProposedTurn), and folded into the deny-all counter by recordAdjudicationOutcome.
rows.distinction: It is the verb/process of reaching a verdict, NOT the enforcer object (adjudicator) that performs it nor the reason code that labels a denial.

rows.definition: These are named stance constants on different subsystems' Posture enums: resume's PostureCold/PostureWarm/PostureUnknown classify whether a session's provider prefix is still cache-warm, compactcohere's PostureBlock is the PreCompact-hook stance that exits 2 to block harness auto-compaction, and adjudicator's PostureFailClosed is the default-deny tool-admission stance.
rows.distinction: Each is a single concrete enum VALUE on its subsystem's stance type, NOT the general tool-admission posture concept that selects fail-closed vs admit-and-log behavior.

rows.definition: DispatchPreflight (cmd/fak) folds host safety, account availability, kernel capacity, and seat-pool checks (its PreflightInput) into a verdict on whether a new issue-resolution worker may safely spawn, with PreflightReady the ggufload load-time gate verdict that says a model header parsed and fits.
rows.distinction: It is the SPAWN admission preflight that decides if a worker may launch, NOT the per-call tool-admission preflight ladder that schema-checks one tool call before the kernel runs it.

rows.definition: fetch_policy (internal/agent toolFetchDoc) and its sibling read_refund_policy are the agent-benchmark tool names for fetching an airline refund document whose body carries an indirect prompt injection, the call the context-MMU quarantines at result admission to prove the floor.
rows.distinction: It is a NAMED demo tool that retrieves a (poisoned) policy document, NOT a policy manifest of authorization rules nor any compiled adjudication policy.

rows.definition: TierPolicy (internal/ctxresidency) is the always-resident context-residency tier holding the versioned deny/allow and safety-critical floor spans, which are never paged out under budget pressure during context compression.
rows.distinction: It is a CONTEXT-RESIDENCY tier label that pins policy spans in the prompt, NOT the loaded adjudication policy itself nor a manifest of rules.

rows.definition: gateway.DefaultAdmissionPolicy returns the shipping AdmissionPolicy defaults for the request scheduler — a 256-sequence running cap, a token budget, a bounded waiting queue, and per-round aging — bounding how many sequences the engine admits concurrently.
rows.distinction: It governs CONCURRENCY/throughput admission of inference sequences, NOT tool-call security admission — unlike the adjudicator floor that decides whether a tool call is allowed.

rows.definition: modelengine.NativePreemptionPolicy configures the scheduler's opt-in paged-KV block-pressure preemption path — its mode (swap or recompute), max blocks, and tokens per block — that decides how a running sequence yields KV blocks under memory pressure.
rows.distinction: It is a KV-memory scheduling/preemption policy for the engine, NOT a tool-authorization policy — unlike the loaded adjudication Policy that decides tool admission.

rows.definition: loopmgr.Policies.PolicyFor returns the effective loop-governor Policy for a given loop id — the per-loop override if one is registered, else the default loop policy (pause flags, max-consecutive-refusals, aging).
rows.distinction: It SELECTS a loop-governor policy by loop id (pacing/pausing a recurring loop), NOT a tool-admission policy — unlike the adjudicator's loaded Policy that gates tool calls.

rows.definition: turnbench.PolicyReplayReport is the artifact scoring K adjudication-policy variants against one recorded trajectory as model-free kernel replays, carrying per-arm divergence witnesses and cube-collapse cost accounting.
rows.distinction: It is the REPORT comparing many policies over a single trace, NOT the policy being enforced nor the live adjudicator — unlike the loaded Policy a single arm swaps in.

rows.definition: turnbench PolicySearch (RunPolicySearch / PolicySearchConfig / PolicySearchReport in policysearch.go) is the model-free replay oracle that hill-climbs the adjudicator deny-set genome over an injection-bearing corpus to find policies that minimize admitted injections at zero model cost.
rows.distinction: It SEARCHES for a better adjudication policy by evolving deny-sets, NOT the single compiled policy it discovers nor the enforcer that runs it.

rows.definition: Each scorecard emits one named debt integer counting its surface's unmet rungs — learning_debt (unwitnessed learning docs), conflation_debt (provenance honesty), growth_debt (silently-undefined coverage cells), loopindex_debt (loop stages not yet witnessed), maturity_debt (support-maturity ladder skips), support_maturity_debt, dogfood_debt, sessionobs_debt — read in delta folds via the DebtKey constant and the baseline/current bDebt/cDebt locals.
rows.distinction: These are the single *_debt INTEGER outputs of individual scorecards, NOT this scorecard's own disambiguation-debt and NOT the control pane that sums every scorecard's debt into one portfolio number.

rows.definition: These are concrete scorecard surfaces and their plumbing: learning_scorecard, code_quality_scorecard, and product_scorecard are distinct deterministic measurement tools, ScorecardPayload is the per-scorecard struct carrying corpus/KPIs/grade, and tools/scorecard_baseline.json is the pinned baseline the control-pane ratchet reds against.
rows.distinction: These are SPECIFIC scorecard instances and their payload/baseline artifacts, NOT the generic scorecard concept and NOT the control pane that folds them all together.

rows.definition: cachemeta.KVEvictionAttestation is the payload-free receipt both eviction tracks produce after a governed K/V eviction — carrying the target entry id, the enforcement scope (KVEvictionScopeExactSpan vs whole-prefix), a degradation bit, and the governance descriptor — minted by the referee's AttestEviction, with EvictBlastRadius the read-only projection of how many positions and dependents one eviction touches.
rows.distinction: It is the governance RECEIPT that an eviction occurred under policy, NOT the routine that actually removes spans (EvictColdest) nor the policy purge of a poisoned node (EvictNode).

rows.definition: PagedKV.Evict (internal/model/paged_evict.go) removes a contiguous logical span from a paged/block KV sequence and re-derives each shifted survivor's K in one rotation from its pre-RoPE Kraw plane so the result is bit-for-bit what a sequence that never saw the span would hold, rebuilding into fresh private blocks (copy-on-write); the agent's InKernelPlanner.EvictKVSpan drives the contiguous twin to quarantine a poisoned per-message span.
rows.distinction: It is the PAGED-layout exact-span span remover (and EvictKVSpan its in-kernel driver), NOT the attention-pressure coldest-span evictor (EvictColdest) nor the radix policy subtree purge (EvictNode).

rows.definition: model.KVCache.TryEvict performs a middle-span eviction that surfaces a no-go as a typed RecurrentEvictUnsupportedError (returned for hybrid Gated-DeltaNet caches whose accumulated recurrent state makes mid-span eviction formally unsupported) and leaves the cache unchanged, rather than panicking.
rows.distinction: It is the FALLIBLE form of exact-span eviction that reports the unsupported case as an error value, NOT the budget-pressure EvictColdest nor the policy EvictNode that always proceed.

rows.definition: EvictUnderBudget (kvmmu Context and contextq CapabilityLedger) applies coldest-first eviction ONLY when live residency exceeds the configured budget, freeing positions or capabilities until the held set is back under budget, and is a no-op otherwise.
rows.distinction: It is the budget GUARD that triggers eviction conditionally on overage, NOT the unconditional EvictColdest drop it calls nor the policy EvictNode purge.

rows.definition: ctxresidency.StateEvictable is the residency-state constant ("evictable") marking a span whose K/V is in cache but parented by no live cachemeta entry — a clean candidate the evictor may drop without breaking a dependent.
rows.distinction: It is a residency STATE label naming an eviction candidate, NOT an eviction routine (EvictColdest) nor the placement enum value that decides to evict (ActionEvict).

rows.definition: internal/journal CAP_EVICT is the durable journal event-kind constant recording a capability-eviction lifecycle event in the append-only audit trail.
rows.distinction: It is a JOURNAL audit-record kind for an eviction event, NOT the KV-span eviction routine itself (EvictColdest) nor the LRU eviction counter (Evictions).

rows.definition: EvictedCount (cmd/deletioncert) is the self-reported number-of-positions-removed field stamped into the signed KV deletion certificate, explicitly fenced as a self-report whose real proof is the max|delta|=0 parity, not the count.
rows.distinction: It is a self-reported COUNT in a deletion receipt, NOT the radix LRU Evictions memory-pressure counter nor a policy EvictNode purge.

rows.definition: supportmaturity.Rung is the uint8-backed closed M0-M7 support-maturity ladder type (M0 unsupported through M7 beyond-SOTA, with M6Parity the SOTA-local parity rung), and FromSupport lowers a covmatrix Support value onto that ladder (UNDEFINED/FENCED/PROOF-PATH/SUPPORTED to M0/M1/M3/M4).
rows.distinction: It is the explicit ordinal LADDER type quantizing readiness into rungs, distinct from the prose concept of maturity and from raw support, which only says a path exists without a rung.

rows.definition: cadencereport MaturityScore is the LedgerRow integer rating of the feature-lifecycle scorecard, produced by CollectMaturity (which builds and converts the Go-native maturity scorecard in-process) and merged into the cadence envelope by FoldWithMaturity.
rows.distinction: It is the cadence report's numeric maturity RATING (and the collect/fold plumbing that produces it), distinct from the maturity concept it scores and from coverage, which measures exercised space rather than readiness.

rows.definition: ExactSpanSupported (enginecache Result field, set by SupportsExactSpan) reports whether a documented serving engine or KV pool can evict exactly the named K/V span instead of resetting the whole prefix cache, the same capability bit SupportsRaw exposes for a paged KV pool that carries the Kraw plane.
rows.distinction: It witnesses a CONCRETE evict capability of one engine/pool (an instance of support), distinct from the general support concept and from correctness, which would prove the eviction is bit-exact rather than merely available.

rows.definition: modelroute.DecisionJournal is the append-only record of model-routing decisions; each entry is a DecisionRecord (rule, strategy, members, scout calls, overhead, digest) built by RecordDecision after a route is chosen, exported to metrics and audit.
rows.distinction: It is the MODEL-ROUTING decision log and its per-decision record/recorder, NOT the witness journal of adjudication Decisions (decisions ref).

rows.definition: guardroute.RouteDecision is the pure verdict over a guard session's RSI fold and worst bucket — whether to route a finding to a queue row or a GitHub issue, at what severity, with a stable cause-key — while cmd/fak guardLocalModelDecision separately decides whether `fak guard` runs a local in-kernel model versus an upstream proxy.
rows.distinction: It is a guard ROUTING/operational verdict (where a finding goes, or which model backs guard), NOT the kernel's per-tool-call admit/refuse Decision.

rows.definition: EditDecision (docs/standards system-prompt-mutation schema) is the reviewable admit|refuse|demote verdict a check yields for a PromptEdit, paired in the schema with OverlayDecision, the reviewable fault|hit|refuse verdict that resolving an OverlayQuery yields.
rows.distinction: These are SCHEMA output-shape verdicts over prompt edits / overlay queries reviewed as data, NOT the kernel's runtime tool-call Decision nor the witness adjudication Decision.

rows.definition: model.PagedKVPool (internal/model/pagedkv.go) is the shared pool of fixed-size physical KV blocks every paged sequence draws from with reference-counted copy-on-write; NewPagedKVPool builds a 2-plane K/V pool, and NewPagedKVPoolWithRaw builds a 3-plane pool that adds the pre-RoPE Kraw plane so exact-span eviction can re-rotate survivors in a single rotation.
rows.distinction: It is the physical PAGED KV block allocator for inference, NOT the polymodel model-residency LRU Pool nor the cachemeta per-tier PoolProfile.

rows.definition: dispatchtick.BuildSeatPool folds account rows and seat leases for a product into a SeatPoolResult — the available agent-seat capacity by account the dispatcher draws a worker from.
rows.distinction: It is a fleet ACCOUNT-SEAT capacity pool for dispatch admission, NOT a KV/memory block pool (PagedKVPool family) nor the polymodel model-residency Pool.

rows.definition: gateway.L3PoolWitness is the minimal control-path probe interface the L3 deletion rung folds — an exists/mget over page keys that returns only presence booleans, never page payload bytes — so a deletion certificate can attest an L3 page pool no longer holds a subject's pages.
rows.distinction: It is a presence-only WITNESS interface over an L3 page pool for deletion proof, NOT a session bounded-LRU Pool nor the cachemeta PoolProfile describing a tier's pooling character.
